summaries.headline: Christopher and Rosaria on the sauce, Carolina in the mire
summaries.text: Christopher’s South Korea nicked a 2-1 win over Carolina’s Czech Republic, while Rosaria had a tidy 2-0 with Mexico against South Africa. That makes Christopher the early front-runner, top on 3 points with the best goal difference, with Rosaria also on 3 and looking rather smug. Spare a thought for Carolina, who’s bottom after that opening wobble. Everyone else is still stuck on the starting blocks, so the rest of the sweepstake is having a proper lie-in.

summaries.headline: Vicki runs riot while Max is in a proper pickle
summaries.text: Vicki was the big winner this round, with the United States of America battering Paraguay 4-1. Meanwhile, Henry and Chris had to share the points after Canada and Bosnia & Herzegovina drew 1-1. That means Vicki stays top overall and looking rather smug, while Christopher and Rosaria are still clinging to the chase. At the other end, Max is the main loser after that heavy defeat, and remains bottom. Carolina is also in the red, while Alex, Emily, Grandad, Jack, Nana, Sara, and Sofia are still waiting for a proper nibble from their teams.

summaries.headline: Scotland nick it, Turkey and Brazil have a stinker
summaries.text: A tidy night for Henry, with Scotland doing the business against Haiti and Qatar earning a draw, so he stays top of the shop. Nana also had a belter as Australia beat Turkey 2-0. At the other end, Chris took the bruising with Turkey falling flat, while Jack and Max both had frustrating draws with Haiti and Switzerland, and Brazil only managed a point. Best overall is still Henry on 5 points. Worst are Alex, Emily, Grandad and Sofia, all stuck on 0, with Carolina not far behind on 0 and a worse goal difference than most.

summaries.headline: Christopher cashes in as Sara and Carolina have a grim afternoon
summaries.text: What a day for Christopher: Germany battered Curaçao 7-1, and he stays top of the lot. Emily also had a belter, with Sweden smashing Tunisia 5-1, though the Netherlands and Japan shared the spoils. At the other end, Sara and Carolina both took proper knocks, with Tunisia and Ecuador coming off second best. Grandad did nick a win with Ivory Coast, which is tidy enough. Overall, Christopher leads the sweepstake on 6 points, with Henry right behind on 5. At the basement, Sofia and Carolina are still on 0, which is a bit bleak.

summaries.headline: Handbags at the back as the draws do the damage
summaries.text: A draw-fest has left a few scratching their heads. Emily, Alex, Max, Sara, Vicki and Sofia all shared the spoils, while Grandad’s Spain could only muster a blank against Vicki’s Cape Verde. The big winner remains Christopher out in front on 6 points, with Emily and Vicki hot on his heels. At the other end, Carolina is still stuck on 0, while Jack, Chris and Sofia are all in the danger mud on 1 point apiece. So it’s Christopher wearing the crown for now, and Carolina doing a fine impression of a rain-soaked programme.

summaries.headline: Grandad top of the pops as Sofia takes a walloping
summaries.text: Big day for Grandad and Nana: Austria and Argentina both won convincingly, so they’re grinning like loons. Max also had a handy boost with Norway’s 4-1 win over Iraq. At the other end, Sofia had a proper nightmare: Jordan, Iraq and Senegal all lost, and her only bright spot was much less bright than that sounds. Carolina’s France also won, keeping her in the mix. Overall, Grandad leads the lot on 7 points, with Nana and Christopher breathing down the necks on 6. Worst off is Sofia, stuck on 1 point and looking distinctly fed up.

summaries.headline: Chris chinks his glass while Henry and Jack take a knock
summaries.text: Colombia gave Chris a tidy 3-1 win over Uzbekistan, and England had a lovely old laugh, 4-2 against Croatia, so Alex is purring. Ghana’s 1-0 squeak over Panama also helped Sara a touch. Meanwhile, Henry and Jack both had a bit of a rough one, with Croatia and Portugal failing to get the job done. At the top, Grandad still leads the lot on 7 points, with Nana and Christopher breathing down his neck on 6. At the other end, Sofia remains rooted to the bottom on 1 point. Not exactly champagne football, that.

summaries.headline: Henry turns the screw as Sofia’s season goes pear-shaped
summaries.text: Henry had the day out, with Canada smashing Qatar 6-0 to keep him top of the pile. Jack also fancied it, as Switzerland beat Bosnia & Herzegovina 4-1. Rosaria got a tidy win too, with Mexico edging South Korea 1-0. At the wrong end, Christopher took a bump, and Chris had a rough one as Bosnia & Herzegovina were punted. Overall, Henry is best with 8 points, while Sofia is bottom with 1 point and looking distinctly wobbly.

summaries.headline: Max and Vicki on the lash, Nana left with the crumbs
summaries.text: Brazil (Max) and USA (Vicki) were the big winners, both bagging clean-sheet wins. Morocco (Sara) also nicked it, leaving Scotland (Henry) with a sore one. At the other end, Jack and Nana had a grim old shift, both beaten without scoring. Overall, Vicki stays top with 8 points and the best goal difference, while Max is right on the case in second on 8. At the bottom, Sofia is still in the mud on 1 point, with Chris and Carolina also needing a proper turnaround.

summaries.headline: Christopher stays in clover as Grandad and Emily take knocks
summaries.text: Christopher had the best of it with Germany beating Ivory Coast 2-1 and Curaçao drawing 0-0, keeping him right in the hunt. Emily had a belter on paper with the Netherlands thumping Sweden 5-1, though Sweden’s the side taking the hiding. Grandad took a bit of a ding from Germany’s win over Ivory Coast. Overall, Max is still top of the pile on 11 points, with Christopher hot on his heels on 10. At the other end, Sofia remains propping up the table on 1 point, with Chris and Carolina also looking a bit ropey.

summaries.headline: Sofia steals the show while Alex and Vicki tread water
summaries.text: Sofia was the big winner, with Egypt beating New Zealand and giving her a tidy boost. The other result worth a cheer was the dead-heat between Uruguay and Cape Verde, which kept Sara and Vicki from going backwards. The damp squib was Belgium and Iran drawing blanks, so Vicki and Emily both missed a chance to climb. Overall, Max is still top dog on 11 points, with Grandad and Vicki snapping at his heels on 10. At the other end, Sofia and Chris are propping up the table on 4 points, with Sofia taking the latest hit.

summaries.headline: Max top of the pile as Sofia and Grandad cop it
summaries.text: A tidy night for Max, with Norway (Max) edging Senegal (Sofia), and that keeps Max out in front overall on 14 points. France (Carolina) gave Iraq (Sofia) a proper hiding, while Argentina (Nana) saw off Austria (Grandad), so Sofia and Grandad both took a bit of a pasting. At the top, Max is still the main boss; at the bottom, Sofia remains stuck in last on 4 points. Not exactly champagne football for the chasing pack, is it?

summaries.headline: Chris gets the spoils as Jack and Emily come unstuck
summaries.text: A tidy night for Chris, who nicked Colombia (Chris) past DR Congo (Jack) to stay on the up. Henry also had a decent one, with Croatia doing the business over Panama (Emily), while Alex had to settle for a goalless snooze against Ghana (Sara). Jack and Emily took the main knocks from the results. Up top, Max is still the class act on 14 points. At the other end, Sofia remains rooted to the foot of the table on 4, which is a bit of a state of affairs.

summaries.headline: Rosaria’s having a belter as Mexico and South Africa keep rolling
summaries.text: Rosaria had a lovely little afternoon: Mexico swatted Czech Republic (Carolina) aside, and South Africa edged South Korea (Christopher), so Rosaria stays right on Max’s heels. Max still had the biggest win of the lot though, with Brazil giving Scotland (Henry) a proper going-over. Henry’s the main loser from the round, with Carolina and Christopher also taking hits. Overall, Max is still best on 17 points, while Sofia is bottom on 4 and looking a touch grim.

summaries.headline: Turkey take the biscuit as Max keeps the lid on the lot
summaries.text: Turkey (Chris) edged USA (Vicki) 3-2, so Chris takes the cheers and Vicki takes the bruises. Elsewhere it was all rather civilised: Paraguay (Max) v Australia (Nana) finished 0-0, and Japan (Alex) v Sweden (Emily) also shared the points. No big wobbles for the leaders, but Max stays top overall on 18 points, with Rosaria next on 16. At the other end, Sofia is still propping up the table on 4 points, a fair way adrift of the pack.

summaries.headline: Max still top, while Norway and Uruguay cop the rough end of it
summaries.text: A tidy little night for Max, who still sits top overall with 19 points, even if Norway were thumped 4-1 by France and only Saudi Arabia scraped a 0-0 with Cape Verde. Grandad had the best of it as Spain beat Uruguay 1-0, keeping him right in the title scrap on 16. Carolina also picked up useful points through France. At the other end, Sara took the biggest wobble after Uruguay’s defeat, while Vicki’s Cape Verde draw did little to shift the mood. Bottom overall remains Sofia on 7 points, with Grandad still the best placed and Max leading the lot.

summaries.headline: Rosaria shares the spoils as Jack misfires
summaries.text: Algeria (Rosaria) and Austria (Grandad) served up a 3-3 thriller, so both got a point and no one got properly stuffed there. Argentina (Nana) did the business, beating Jordan (Sofia) 3-1, with Sofia the day’s clear loser. Colombia (Chris) and Portugal (Jack) played out a blank, which is a bit of a snooze for Jack. Overall, Max is still top on 19 points, with Grandad and Rosaria breathing down the necks on 17. At the other end, Sofia is still bottom on 8, looking like the sweepstake’s resident club shop window.

summaries.headline: Henry’s late nick, Grandad held, Sofia takes a shellacking
summaries.text: Henry got the big boost with Canada (Henry) edging South Africa (Rosaria), while Argentina (Nana) had the better of Jordan (Sofia). The draw between Algeria (Rosaria) and Austria (Grandad) was a mixed bag: Grandad stayed near the sharp end, but Rosaria missed a chance to pull clear. Overall, Max is still top on 19 points and looking rather smug. At the other end, Sofia remains bottom on 8 points and needs a miracle and a strong cup of tea.

summaries.headline: Max keeps the crown as Alex and Henry feel the pinch
summaries.text: Brazil (Max) edged Japan (Alex) 2-1, so Max stays top and Alex takes the hit. Canada (Henry) then won 1-0 against South Africa (Rosaria), giving Henry a handy boost and leaving Rosaria with a frustrating week. The only draw was Algeria (Rosaria) 3-3 Austria (Grandad), which kept Grandad steady but did Rosaria no favours. Overall, Max is still best with 22 points, while Sofia remains bottom on 8. Henry and Rosaria are bunched on 17, but Henry’s goal difference is the shakiest of the lot.

summaries.headline: Max marches on while Carolina and Grandad get the rough end
summaries.text: A tidy little round for Max, with Norway (Max) winning 2-1 and keeping the top spot comfy. Rosaria also had a belter as Mexico (Rosaria) beat Ecuador (Carolina) 2-0, and Carolina took another wobble with France (Carolina) losing 3-0 to Sweden (Emily). Grandad’s day went a bit pear-shaped too, thanks to Ivory Coast (Grandad) going down 2-1 to Norway (Max). Overall, Max is still best on 26 points, while Sofia is propping up the lot on 8. A proper scrap in the middle, that.
